attacking_style: Early breakaway specialist who attacks from the gun or launches surge moves 15-25km from finish on climbs. Frequently initiates winning moves but struggles to finish them, blowing up in finale accelerations.
team_tactics: EF uses him as both protected GC rider and breakaway infiltrator. Often rides solo or with minimal domestique support. Coordination with teammates (JORGENSON, MARTÍNEZ) in breaks to control pace or defend secondary jerseys.
terrain_preference: Selective climbs and medium mountain stages where positioning trumps pure watts. Excels in breakaway survival on rolling/hilly terrain. Monuments and one-week stage races suit his resilience. Struggles on explosive summit finishes and punchy climbs like Mur de Huy.
vulnerability: Cannot follow final accelerations when pure climbers attack (AYUSO example). Goes too early on climbs and blows up before finish. Invisible on steep, explosive climbs. Lacks cooperation leverage in tactical breakaway negotiations (CLARKE incident).
key_patterns: Attacks from gun or enters early breaks, observed across 2020-2023 Tours, Paris-Nice, Suisse (6+ instances).
Initiates winning moves but fades in finale, blowing up to 4th-5th place (Tour 2020, Suisse 2023).
KOM jersey defender through calculated point-trading rather than stage wins (Tour 2023, three observations).
Fourth-place Monument finish from breakaway shows exceptional resilience positioning (Flanders 2023).
Functions as breakaway domestique for teammates' GC or stage wins (MARTÍNEZ 2020, JORGENSON 2023).
Mechanical issues disrupt promising GC campaigns multiple times (Suisse 2022, Paris-Nice 2020).
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 showed tactical maturity (KOM defense, Flanders 4th) but couldn't convert attacks to wins. Disappeared at Flèche. Consistent aggression without breakthrough results suggests plateau.
built_from: 2023 Ronde van Vlaanderen - Tour des Flandres
